---
title: "1.4 Assignment and Input"
subtitle: "Part 3: Dog Years Coding Challenge"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Dog Years

::: {.image-fit}
![Dog](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-4-assignment-and-input/dog-free.png){fig-align="center" width="22%"}
:::

In this coding challenge, you will calculate your age, and your pet's age from your birthdates, and your pet's age in dog years.

## Challenge Prompt

### `activecode:: challenge1-4`

In the code, type in:

::: {.tight-list}
- the current year
- the year you were born
- the year your dog or cat was born
:::

If you do not have a dog or cat, make one up.

## Challenge Prompt

### Formulas and output

Write formulas in assignment statements to calculate:

::: {.tight-list}
- how old you are
- how old your dog or cat is
- how old they are in dog years, which is `7` times a human year
:::

Finally, print it all out.

If you are pair programming, switch drivers after every line of code.

## Starter Code

### `activecode:: challenge1-4`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Challenge1_4
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // Fill in values for these variables
        int currentYear =
        int birthYear =
        int dogBirthYear =

        // Write a formula to calculate your age from the currentYear and
        // your birthYear variables
        int age =

        // Write a formula to calculate your dog's age from the currentYear
        // and dogBirthYear variables
        int dogAge =

        // Calculate the age of your dog in dogYears (7 times your dog's age
        // in human years)
        int dogYearsAge =

        // Print out your age, your dog's age, and your dog's age in dog
        // years. Make sure you print out text too so that the user knows what
        // is being printed out.

   }
}
```
:::

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: challenge1-4`

Runestone checks that you initialized the three variables:

::: {.tight-list}
- `int currentYear =` followed by a 2-4 digit value
- `int birthYear =` followed by a 2-4 digit value
- `int dogBirthYear =` followed by a 1-4 digit value
:::

It also checks that the program has some output and at least one `System.out.print` statement.

## Test Requirements

### Required formulas

Runestone checks that the code contains these formulas:

```java
age = currentYear - birthYear;
dogAge = currentYear - dogBirthYear;
dogYearsAge = dogAge * 7;
```

It also accepts:

```java
dogYearsAge = 7 * dogAge;
```

## Optional Extension

### Interactive input

Your teacher may suggest that you use a Java IDE with interactive input using the `Scanner` class for this challenge.

The source lists JuiceMind/Replit templates and a Scanner link for this optional path.

This static deck keeps the textbook starter code and test requirements as the required classroom task.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- initialize `currentYear`, `birthYear`, and `dogBirthYear`
- calculate `age` from `currentYear - birthYear`
- calculate `dogAge` from `currentYear - dogBirthYear`
- calculate `dogYearsAge` from `dogAge * 7` or `7 * dogAge`
- print text labels with the numeric results
- preserve the starter-code variables so the source tests can find them
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
